fees.qualifier: One-time allowance applied toward the following implementation services, expiring 90 days post go-live: custom programming including file conversions; customization of consumer portal; custom communications including formulary notifications; custom ID cards; customization of standard reports; on-site training; open refill and other file-feed costs from the incumbent vendor; pre- or post-implementation audit allowance.

Allowance used during the contract period, applied toward: drug-file access fee; file conversions requiring custom programming (ongoing maintenance); prescriber panel or facility configuration; customization of consumer portal; custom communications; customization of standard reports; approved clinical programs; on-site training.

fees.service_name: Manual / hard-copy eligibility maintenance — member and/or physician update
Manual / hard-copy eligibility maintenance — group and/or division update
Custom IT programming (including ad-hoc report development)
Eligibility and plan benefit support — late fee
fees.qualifier: $235 per hour for IT time to meet the original implementation or change date
Initial value ID cards included (2 logos, 2 cards per household at no charge). Electronic ID cards available via consumer portal. Fees may apply for additional and replacement cards.
$0.03 per claim — standard USPS delivery; pass-through cost for overnight / specialty delivery

fees.service_name: Standard FWA program (desktop and on-site audit, 100% pass-through of audit recoveries)
Comprehensive clinical trend management (quantity limit, step therapy, prior-auth edits)

fees.qualifier: per-vaccine claim service fee; vaccine cost and pharmacy administration fee billed separately

fees.service_name: Reprocessing of claims / retroactive claim adjustments

included_services.description: Implementation services and timeline
Prescription benefit design implementation and management services
Northwind standard formulary administration
Northwind rebate program
Maximum allowable cost (MAC) program
Electronic prescription claim processing and payment
Point-of-sale online messaging
Pharmacy network administration, contracting, auditing, and credentialing
Real-time concurrent drug utilization review safety messaging to pharmacy
Toll-free 24/7/365 pharmacy help desk
Claims payment detail sent to participating pharmacies
Standard welcome packet and initial ID cards
Toll-free 24/7/365 customer service center for members, providers, and pharmacists
Operational (administrative) prior authorizations / overrides
Consumer portal (member website and mobile application)
Pharmacist availability 24/7/365 for mail and specialty members
Designated account management team (account executive, account manager, clinical program manager)
Business financial reviews including clinical recommendations
Self-service access to the claims processing portal (real-time eligibility, prior-auth management, claims history)
Self-service access to the standard and ad-hoc reporting portal

assumptions.text: Pricing is based on a minimum of 1,000 lives. If eligible members drop below 1,000 after the first or any subsequent month of the term, materially affecting the ability to achieve financial guarantees, pricing is subject to change.

Financials are based on Northwind's book of business at the time of this proposal. Northwind reserves the right to revisit the financial guarantees if utilization is materially different than the underlying assumptions.

The pharmacy network guarantees specified are the discounts and dispensing fees the Client has agreed to pay; these may be greater or less than actual reimbursement to participating pharmacies, and Northwind retains any difference as part of total compensation.

Network performance is guaranteed on an individual-component basis (ingredient cost and dispensing fee separately), reported quarterly and reconciled annually, with any shortfall covered dollar-for-dollar and credited within 90 days after year-end.
Northwind manages the pharmacy network, including which pharmacies are included and all pharmacy-side contract negotiations and terms.
Retail-90 pricing applies to claims filled with 84+ days' supply; mail pricing assumes an average days' supply of 84 or greater.
Single-source generics are included in the overall generic effective rate.
Zero-balance claims are measured at the discounted price, not at 100% discount.

Network exclusions: OTC, vaccine, compound, 340B, paper (DMR), COB, VA hospital, subrogation, client-contracted pharmacies not at network rates, non-FDA-approved drugs, client-owned pharmacies, and claims with state-determined rates.
Pricing assumes no more than 5% of claims are filled in MA, HI, AK, and PR.
One national retail pharmacy chain is excluded from the retail specialty network.
Rebate guarantees are based on use of the Performance exclusionary formulary with the medium edit package.
Guarantees may be adjusted if specialty volume decreases more than 10% during the term versus prior utilization.
Specialty guarantees are based on standard specialty drugs as identified on the standard Specialty Drug List.
Rebate guarantees apply per federal-legend brand drug identified using standard drug-classification indicators and standard policies for FDA-approved prescription products.

Rebate exclusions: OTC, vaccine/immunization, compound, 340B, member-submitted (DMR), secondary-payer (COB), federal-supply-schedule-eligible, subrogation, 100% member-copay (cash card), non-drug (excluding diabetic test strips), claims older than 180 days, exception-logic claims, repackaged NDC, out-of-network claims, insulin, manufacturer price-reduction/discontinued-rebate products, COVID-related claims, and brand thyroid agents.
Mail and specialty rebate guarantees are contingent on use of the vendor's direct mail and specialty pharmacy; guarantees may be adjusted if a different vendor is elected.
Guarantees may be adjusted for non-standard programs that negatively impact rebates, or if utilization is less than 85.0% market-share compliant with preferred formulary products.
Rebates are guaranteed per channel.
Rebates are applied to brand discounts and assume use of the Performance formulary with the medium edit package.
Specialty guarantees may be adjusted if specialty volume decreases more than 10% during the term versus prior utilization.
Specialty rebates are applied to specialty brand discounts based on the standard Specialty Drug List.
Rebates are contingent on use of the vendor's direct mail and specialty pharmacy and may be adjusted if a different vendor is elected.
